generatedSentence: The city buzzed with `tremors`, an `itchy warning` of impending doom. `Arrogant dealers`, with their `outlandish posture`, controlled `sketchy trails` of `junked` tech, operating without official `sanction`. Their `bully` tactics `implied` a deep `volatility`. `Hearsay` suggested `nephews` were `racking` up debts, while `guards` `revamped` old systems, `hammers` clanging. A `barcode` `adjoins` the `goldfish` pond, a strange detail to an otherwise `abandon`ed district. A `little` `stargazer`, whose `honest` `intellect` `prevents` him from falling for `fairytale` promises, observed the `backbreaking` work of `bankers` and their `military` `layout` near the `railroad`. He knew the `grosses` profits `juggles` by the `doomed` syndicate, shielded by `tinfoil` and `turbine` power, creating `nuclear` ambitions. He felt `whacked` by the absurdity, his `wryneck` from constantly looking up at the `vacancy` of space, knowing `victims` were `drunkenly` caught in the web. Under the `nighttime` `skyscraper`, the `ickiest` `inky` `imports` arrived, `heavily` guarded. A `tapir`-like `android` stood sentinel, its `horizontal` gaze `streaky` with reflected neon. The `jockeys` `gloated` as they `openly` `wander`ed through `barrels` of goods, mocking `decency` and `donations`. An `incredibly` `artsy` `chandelier`, a strange beacon in this `safari` of depravity, hung above. `Blimey`, it was a scene where `anybody` could be `bitten` by the `legalities` or `chemistry` of their `puppets`, struggling to `revive` hope. Fortunately, an `arbiter` from a secret `league` had been watching, mapping the `ramifications` through the `vortexes` of information. He wore a simple `hat`, a `sunbeam` glinting `through` the `arctic` chill of the data stream. `Instantly`, he knew the `lowdown` that caused such `anguish` and `anarchy`. With a `nerve` of steel, he prepared to `launch` an attack using the old `railways`, aiming to `scissor` through their control, protecting the `numerous jewels` of freedom and `honourable` citizens before `lockjaw` seized the entire city.

He knew the `grosses` profits `juggles` by the `doomed` syndicate, shielded by `tinfoil` and `turbine` power, creating `nuclear` ambitions. imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. Protagonist: a lone figure with a cybernetic eyepatch and a flowing, dark trench coat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: The lone figure stands silhouetted against a sprawling, junked cityscape under a volatile, cloudy sky, looking at a distant, glowing skyscraper. Extreme wide shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: The figure is seen from a low angle, ascending a rickety metal staircase adjacent to a massive, derelict turbine, a single ray of light catching their cybernetic eyepatch. Medium shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The figure, now in a dimly lit, futuristic market filled with strange goods in barrels, discreetly observes a group of shady figures in the foreground. Over-the-shoulder shot. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The figure, now on a high-speed maglev train, gazes out at a neon-streaked digital map projected onto the window, showing lines converging on the skyscraper. Medium shot, dynamic angle.

definitions.definition: A shake, quiver, or vibration.
definitions.example: She felt a tremor in her stomach before going on stage.

definitions.definition: An earthquake.
definitions.example: Did you feel the tremor this morning?

definitions.definition: To shake or quiver excessively and rapidly or involuntarily; to tremble.
Having or creating an itch, causing a person or animal to tend to want to scratch.
To make (someone) aware of (something impending); especially:
To caution or admonish (someone) against unwise or unacceptable behaviour.
definitions.example: Don't let me catch you running in the corridor again, I warn you.

definitions.definition: (chiefly with "off", "away", and similar words) To advise or order to go or stay away.
definitions.example: A sign warns trespassers off/away from the site.

definitions.definition: To give warning.
The action of the verb warn; an instance of warning someone.
Something spoken or written that is intended to warn.
definitions.example: The boss gave him a warning that he would be fired if he did not desist from his behaviour.

definitions.definition: Used to warn of danger in signs and notices.
The way a person holds and positions their body.
A situation or condition.
One's attitude or the social or political position one takes towards an issue or another person.
The position of someone or something relative to another; position; situation.
To put one's body into a posture or series of postures, especially hoping that one will be noticed and admired
definitions.example: If you're finished posturing in front of the mirror, can I use the bathroom now?

definitions.definition: To pretend to have an opinion or a conviction
definitions.example: The politicians couldn't really care less about the issue: they're just posturing for the media.

definitions.definition: To place in a particular position or attitude; to pose.
definitions.example: to posture oneself; to posture a model

definitions.definition: One who deals in goods, especially automobiles; a middleman.
definitions.example: That used car dealer gave me a great deal on my 1962 rusted-out Volkswagen bug!

definitions.definition: A drug dealer, one who peddles illicit drugs.
A particular type of stock broker or trader.
The person who deals the cards in a card game.
The track or indication marking the route followed by something that has passed, such as the footprints of animal on land or the contrail of an airplane in the sky.
A route for travel over land, especially a narrow, unpaved pathway for use by hikers, horseback riders, etc.
A trailer broadcast on television for a forthcoming film or programme.
A walk in which all the edges are distinct.
To follow behind (someone or something); to tail (someone or something).
definitions.example: The hunters trailed their prey deep into the woods.

definitions.definition: To drag (something) behind on the ground.
definitions.example: You'll get your coat all muddy if you trail it around like that.

definitions.definition: To leave (a trail of).
definitions.example: He walked into the house, soaking wet, and trailed water all over the place.

definitions.definition: To show a trailer of (a film, TV show etc.); to release or publish a preview of (a report etc.) in advance of the full publication.
definitions.example: His new film was trailed on TV last night.

definitions.definition: To hang or drag loosely behind; to move with a slow sweeping motion.
definitions.example: The bride's long dress trailed behind her as she walked down the aisle.

definitions.definition: To run or climb like certain plants.
To drag oneself lazily or reluctantly along.
definitions.example: Our parents marched to church and we trailed behind.

definitions.definition: To be losing, to be behind in a competition.
To carry (a firearm) with the breech near the ground and the upper part inclined forward, the piece being held by the right hand near the middle.
To flatten (grass, etc.) by walking through it; to tread down.
To take advantage of the ignorance of; to impose upon.
An approval, by an authority, generally one that makes something valid.
definitions.example: The whalers had been operating in the contested waters off the island with sanction from the Japanese government.

definitions.definition: A penalty, punishment, or some coercive measure, intended to ensure compliance; especially one adopted by several nations, or by an international body.
definitions.example: The United States enacted a new round of sanctions against the apartheid regime of South Africa.

definitions.definition: A law, treaty, or contract, or a clause within a law, treaty, or contract, specifying any of the above.
To ratify; to make valid.
To give official authorization or approval to; to countenance.
To penalize (a State etc.) with sanctions.
To surpass in speaking; say or express more than; signify or claim superiority to; be superior to in meaning or significance; speak louder than.
To speak out or aloud.
To declare; utter; express; vocalise.
Speaking, or spoken, freely, openly, or boldly; vocal; frank.
definitions.example: an outspoken man

definitions.definition: Bizarre, strange
definitions.example: The rock star wore black with outlandish pink and green spiked hair.

definitions.definition: (of a proposition) to have as a necessary consequence
definitions.example: The proposition that "all dogs are mammals" implies that my dog is a mammal

definitions.definition: (of a person) to suggest by logical inference
definitions.example: When I state that your dog is brown, I am not implying that all dogs are brown

definitions.definition: (of a person or proposition) to hint; to insinuate; to suggest tacitly and avoid a direct statement
To enfold, entangle.
The state of being volatile
A son of one's sibling, brother-in-law, or sister-in-law; either a son of one's brother (fraternal nephew) or a son of one's sister (sororal nephew).
A son of one's child.
To throw away.
To find something for very little money (meaning derived from the term junk shop)
definitions.example: (On Facebook, a record collector wrote:) "The newest addition to my Annette Hanshaw collection, I junked this beautiful flawless E-copy within walking distance from my house."

definitions.definition: Information that was heard by one person about another that cannot be adequately substantiated.
Evidence based on the reports of others, which is normally inadmissible because it was not made under oath, rather than on personal knowledge.

An out-of-court statement offered in court for the truth of the matter asserted, which is normally inadmissible because it is not subject to cross-examination unless the hearsay statement falls under one of a number of exceptions.
A tool with a heavy head and a handle used for pounding.
definitions.example: Bobby used a hammer and nails to fix the two planks together

definitions.definition: The act of using a hammer to hit something}}
definitions.example: The nail is too loose - give it a hammer.

definitions.definition: A moving part of a firearm that strikes the firing pin to discharge a gun.
The malleus, a small bone of the middle ear.
In a piano or dulcimer, a piece of wood covered in felt that strikes the string.
definitions.example: The sound the piano makes comes from the hammers striking the strings

definitions.definition: A device made of a heavy steel ball attached to a length of wire, and used for throwing.
The last stone in an end.
(frisbee) A frisbee throwing style in which the disc is held upside-down with a forehand grip and thrown above the head.
Part of a clock that strikes upon a bell to indicate the hour.
One who, or that which, smites or shatters.
definitions.example: St. Augustine was the hammer of heresies.

definitions.definition: To strike repeatedly with a hammer, some other implement, the fist, etc.
definitions.example: Tony hammered on the door to try to get him to open.

definitions.definition: To form or forge with a hammer; to shape by beating.
To emphasize a point repeatedly.
To hit particularly hard.
To ride very fast.
To strike internally, as if hit by a hammer.
definitions.example: I could hear the engine’s valves hammering once the timing rod was thrown.

definitions.definition: To defeat (a person, a team) resoundingly
definitions.example: We hammered them 5-0!

definitions.definition: To make high demands on (a system or service).
To declare (a person) a defaulter on the stock exchange.
To beat down the price of (a stock), or depress (a market).
To have hard sex with
definitions.example: Daniel hammered Mary til he came.

definitions.definition: The faculty of thinking, judging, abstract reasoning, and conceptual understanding; the cognitive faculty (uncountable)
definitions.example: Intellect is one of man's greatest powers.

definitions.definition: The capacity of that faculty (in a particular person) (uncountable)
definitions.example: They were chosen because of their outstanding intellect.

definitions.definition: A person who has that faculty to a great degree
definitions.example: Some of the world's leading intellects were meeting there.

definitions.definition: To place in or hang on a rack.
To torture (someone) on the rack.
To cause (someone) to suffer pain.
To stretch or strain; to harass, or oppress by extortion.
To put the balls into the triangular rack and set them in place on the table.
To strike a male in the testicles.
To (manually) load (a round of ammunition) from the magazine or belt into firing position in an automatic or semiautomatic firearm.
To move the slide bar on a shotgun in order to chamber the next round.
To wash (metals, ore, etc.) on a rack.
To bind together, as two ropes, with cross turns of yarn, marline, etc.
(structural engineering) Tending to shear a structure (that is, force it to move in different directions at different points).
definitions.example: Post-and-lintel construction racks easily.

definitions.definition: To stretch a person's joints.
To drive; move; go forward rapidly; stir
To fly, as vapour or broken clouds
To clarify, and thereby deter further fermentation of, beer, wine or cider by draining or siphoning it from the dregs.
(of a horse) To amble fast, causing a rocking or swaying motion of the body; to pace.
(roofing) A method of asphalt shingle application, whereby shingle courses are applied vertically, up the roof rather than laterally across and up.
Spun yarn used in racking ropes
The process of clarifying, and thereby deterring further fermentation of, beer, wine or cider by draining or siphoning it from the dregs.
To renovate, revise, improve or renew.
definitions.example: They plan to revamp the historical theater in the old downtown.

definitions.definition: A person who, or thing that, protects or watches over something.
definitions.example: The prison guard unlocked the door of the cell.   After completing the repairs, he replaced the sump guard.

definitions.definition: A garda; a police officer.
A squad responsible for protecting something.
definitions.example: The president inspected the guard of honour.

definitions.definition: The part of a sword that protects the wielder's hand.
A part of a machine which blocks access to dangerous parts.
definitions.example: The motorcycle mechanic removed the damaged chain guard.

definitions.definition: A watchchain.
A panel of a car that encloses the wheel area, especially the front wheels.
A state of caution; posture of defence.
Something worn to protect part of the body, e.g. the shins in cricket.
A relatively short player, playing farther from the basket than a forward or center.
The position on the popping crease where a batsman makes a mark to align himself with the wicket; see take guard.

Either of two offensive positions between the center and each of the offensive tackles, whose main responsibilities are to protect the quarterback, and open up "holes" through which offensive players can run.
A player playing a position named guard.
An employee, normally travelling in the last vehicle of a train, responsible for the safety of the train.
A Boolean expression that must evaluate to true for a branch of program execution to continue.
To protect from danger; to secure against surprise, attack, or injury; to keep in safety; to defend.
To keep watch over, in order to prevent escape or restrain from acts of violence, or the like.
definitions.example: Guard the prisoner.

definitions.definition: To watch by way of caution or defense; to be caution; to be in a state or position of defense or safety.
definitions.example: Careful people guard against mistakes.

definitions.definition: To protect the edge of, especially with an ornamental border; hence, to face or ornament with lists, laces, etc.
To fasten by binding; to gird.
A type of small fish, Carassius auratus, typically orange-colored.
definitions.example: For the children's fair we'll have a few games they can play, so that each child "wins" a goldfish as a prize.

definitions.definition: To give up or relinquish control of, to surrender or to give oneself over, or to yield to one's emotions.
To desist in doing, practicing, following, holding, or adhering to; to turn away from; to permit to lapse; to renounce; to discontinue.
To leave behind; to desert as in a ship or a position, typically in response to overwhelming odds or impending dangers; to forsake, in spite of a duty or responsibility.
definitions.example: Many baby girls have been abandoned on the streets of Beijing.

definitions.definition: To subdue; to take control of.
To cast out; to banish; to expel; to reject.
To no longer exercise a right, title, or interest, especially with no interest of reclaiming it again; to yield; to relinquish.
To surrender to the insurer (an insured item), so as to claim a total loss.
Having excessive pride in oneself, often with contempt or disrespect for others.
To be in contact or connection with.
definitions.example: The living room and dining room adjoin each other.

definitions.definition: To extend an algebraic object (e.g. a field, a ring etc.) by adding to it (an element not belonging to it) and all finite power series of (the element).
Any set of machine-readable parallel bars or concentric circles, varying in width, spacing, or height, encoding information according to a symbology
A portion of a gene that identifies a particular species
A person who is intentionally, physically, or emotionally cruel to others; especially to those who are weaker or have less power or privilege.
definitions.example: A playground bully pushed a girl off the swing.

definitions.definition: A noisy, blustering fellow, more insolent than courageous; one who is threatening and quarrelsome; an insolent, tyrannical fellow.
A hired thug.
A sex worker’s minder.
Bully beef.
A brisk, dashing fellow.
The small scrum in the Eton College field game.
Various small freshwater or brackishwater fish of the family Eleotridae; sleeper goby.
An (eldest) brother; a fellow workman; comrade
A companion; mate (male or female).
A darling, sweetheart (male or female).
A standoff between two players from the opposing teams, who repeatedly hit each other's hockey sticks and then attempt to acquire the ball, as a method of resuming the game in certain circumstances.
A miner's hammer.
To intimidate (someone) as a bully.
definitions.example: You shouldn't bully people for being gay.

definitions.definition: To act aggressively towards.
Very good.
definitions.example: a bully horse

definitions.definition: Jovial and blustering.
(often followed by for) Well done!
definitions.example: Bully, she's finally asked for that promotion!

definitions.definition: Roughly or hastily laid out; intended for later refinement.
definitions.example: The first draft included a sketchy design.

definitions.definition: Resembling a comedy sketch, of sketch quality.
Of questionable or doubtful quality.
definitions.example: The sketchy repair job did not look like it would hold.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Suspected of taking part in illicit or dishonorable dealings.
definitions.example: Because he is so sketchy, I always think that he is up to something.

definitions.definition: (of a person) Disturbing or unnerving, often in such a way that others may suspect them of intending physical or sexual harm or harassment.
definitions.example: Jack is so sketchy. I think he's stalking me.

definitions.definition: A small amount.
definitions.example: Can I try a little of that sauce?

definitions.definition: Small in size.
definitions.example: This is a little table.

definitions.definition: Insignificant, trivial.
definitions.example: It's of little importance.

definitions.definition: Very young.
definitions.example: Did he tell you any embarrassing stories about when she was little?

definitions.definition: (of a sibling) Younger.
definitions.example: This is my little sister.

definitions.definition: (also Little) Used with the name of a place, especially of a country or its capital, to denote a neighborhood whose residents or storekeepers are from that place.
Small in amount or number, having few members.
definitions.example: little money;  little herd

definitions.definition: Short in duration; brief.
definitions.example: I feel better after my little sleep.

definitions.definition: Small in extent of views or sympathies; narrow; shallow; contracted; mean; illiberal; ungenerous.
Not much.
definitions.example: This is a little known fact.  She spoke little and listened less.

definitions.definition: Not at all.
definitions.example: I was speaking ill of Fred; little did I know that he was right behind me, listening in.

definitions.definition: Not much; not a large amount.
definitions.example: Little is known about his early life.

definitions.definition: (of a liquid) Giving off bubbles; fizzy.
Vivacious and enthusiastic.
To stop (an outcome); to keep from (doing something).
definitions.example: I brush my teeth regularly to prevent them from turning yellow.

definitions.definition: To take preventative measures.
To come before; to precede.
To outdo, surpass.
To be beforehand with; to anticipate.
A permanent road consisting of fixed metal rails to drive trains or similar motorized vehicles on.
definitions.example: Many railroads roughly follow the trace of older land - and/or water roads

definitions.definition: The transportation system comprising such roads and vehicles fitted to travel on the rails, usually with several vehicles connected together in a train.
A single, privately or publicly owned property comprising one or more such roads and usually associated assets
definitions.example: Railroads can only compete fully if their tracks are technically compatible with and linked to each-other

definitions.definition: A procedure conducted in haste without due consideration.
definitions.example: The lawyers made the procedure a railroad to get the signatures they needed.

definitions.definition: To transport via railroad.
To operate a railroad.
definitions.example: The Thatcherite experiment proved the private sector can railroad as inefficiently as a state monopoly

definitions.definition: To work for a railroad.
To travel by railroad.
To engage in a hobby pertaining to railroads.
To manipulate and hasten a procedure, as of formal approval of a law or resolution.
definitions.example: The majority railroaded the bill through parliament, without the customary expert studies which would delay it till after the elections.

definitions.definition: To convict of a crime by circumventing due process.
definitions.example: They could only convict him by railroading him on suspect drug-possession charges.

definitions.definition: To procedurally bully someone into an unfair agreement.
definitions.example: He was railroaded into signing a non-disclosure agreement at his exit interview.

definitions.definition: To force characters to complete a task before allowing the plot to continue.
(upholstery) To run fabric horizontally instead of the usual vertically.
A folktale featuring fairies or similar fantasy characters.
definitions.example: I used to love reading fairy tales when I was a child.

definitions.definition: A story presented as true that is not believable.
definitions.example: He's telling us another fairy tale about how great the software will be.

definitions.definition: Of the nature of a fairy tale; as if from a fairy tale.
definitions.example: a fairy-tale romance

definitions.definition: Twelve dozen = 144.
The total nominal earnings or amount, before taxes, expenses, exceptions or similar are deducted. That which remains after all deductions is called net.
The bulk, the mass, the masses.
To earn money, not including expenses.
definitions.example: The movie grossed three million on the first weekend.

definitions.definition: Nuclear power
Pertaining to the nucleus of an atom.
Involving energy released by nuclear reactions (fission, fusion, radioactive decay).
definitions.example: a nuclear reactor

definitions.definition: Relating to a weapon that derives its force from rapid release of energy through nuclear reactions.
definitions.example: a nuclear explosion

definitions.definition: (by extension, metaphoric, of a solution or response) Involving an extreme course of action.
definitions.example: nuclear option, nuclear solution

definitions.definition: Pertaining to the nucleus of a cell.
Pertaining to a centre around which something is developed or organised; central, pivotal.
(juggling) The act of throwing and catching each prop at least twice, as opposed to a flash.
The handling or managing of many tasks at once.
The performance of a magic trick.
A deceit or imposture.

To manipulate objects, such as balls, clubs, beanbags, rings, etc. in an artful or artistic manner. Juggling may also include assorted other circus skills such as the diabolo, devil sticks, hat, and cigar box manipulation as well.
definitions.example: She can juggle flaming torches.

definitions.definition: To handle or manage many tasks at once.
definitions.example: He juggled home, school, and work for two years.

definitions.definition: To deceive by trick or artifice.
To joke or jest.
To perform magic tricks.
A thin, pliable sheet of tin or an alloy of tin and lead, used as a protective wrapping.
Aluminium foil
To cover in tinfoil.
Any of various rotary machines that use the kinetic energy of a continuous stream of fluid (a liquid or a gas) to turn a shaft.
Of work, very physically tiring.
One who conducts the business of banking; one who, individually, or as a member of a company, keeps an establishment for the deposit or loan of money, or for traffic in money, bills of exchange, etc.
A money changer.
The dealer, or one who keeps the bank in a gambling house.
The stone bench on which a mason cuts or squares his work.
A vessel employed in the cod fishery on the banks of Newfoundland.
A ditcher; a drain digger.
A banksman.
A railway locomotive that can be attached to the rear of a train to assist it in climbing an incline.
One who stargazes.
A perciform fish in the family Uranoscopidae.
A single step in a staircase.
A series of steps; a staircase.
A contiguous set of steps connecting two floors.
To adorn or grace; to honour; to make becoming, appropriate, or honourable.
(of a person or institution) Scrupulous with regard to telling the truth; not given to swindling, lying, or fraud; upright.
definitions.example: We’re the most honest people you will ever come across.

definitions.definition: (of a statement) True, especially as far as is known by the person making the statement; fair; unbiased.
definitions.example: an honest account of events

definitions.definition: In good faith; without malice.
definitions.example: an honest mistake

definitions.definition: (of a measurement device) Accurate.
definitions.example: an honest scale

definitions.definition: Authentic; full.
definitions.example: an honest day’s work

definitions.definition: Earned or acquired in a fair manner.
definitions.example: an honest dollar

definitions.definition: Open; frank.
definitions.example: an honest countenance

definitions.definition: Decent; honourable; suitable; becoming.
Chaste; faithful; virtuous.
Honestly; really.
definitions.example: It wasn’t my fault, honest.

definitions.definition: To hit, slap or strike.
To kill, bump off.
To share or parcel out; often with up.
definitions.example: to whack the spoils of a robbery

definitions.definition: To beat convincingly; to thrash.
(usually in the negative) To surpass; to better.
Tired; fatigued.
definitions.example: After that all-night party, we were all whacked.

definitions.definition: Intoxicated on drugs.
Either of two small woodpeckers, Jynx torquilla and Jynx ruficollis, of the Old World, that turn their heads almost 180 degrees when foraging.
A twisted or distorted neck; a deformity in which the neck is drawn to one side by a rigid contraction of one of the muscles; torticollis.
A structured arrangement of items within certain limits.
A plan for such arrangement.
The act of laying out something.
The process of arranging editorial content, advertising, graphics and other information to fit within certain constraints.
A map or a drawing of a construction site showing the position of roads, buildings or other constructions.
A specification of an integrated circuit showing the position of the physical components that will implement the schematic in silicon.
The plant Rubus idaeus.
Any of many other (but not all) species in the genus Rubus.
The juicy aggregate fruit of these plants.
A red colour, the colour of a ripe raspberry.
To gather or forage for raspberries.
Containing or having the flavor/flavour of raspberries.
Of a dark pinkish red.
Armed forces.
definitions.example: He spent six years in the military.

definitions.definition: Characteristic of members of the armed forces.
definitions.example: She was dishonorably discharged from all military duties.

definitions.definition: Relating to armed forces such as the army, marines, navy and air force (often as distinguished from civilians or police forces).
definitions.example: If you join a military force, you may end up killing people.

definitions.definition: Relating to war.
Relating to armies or ground forces.
To pronounce sentence or judgment on; to condemn.
definitions.example: a criminal doomed to death

definitions.definition: To destine; to fix irrevocably the ill fate of.
To judge; to estimate or determine as a judge.
To ordain as a penalty; hence, to mulct or fine.
To assess a tax upon, by estimate or at discretion.
Assured to suffer death, failure, or a similarly negative outcome.
In a drunken manner
One that is harmed—killed, injured, subjected to oppression, deceived, or otherwise adversely affected—by someone or something, especially another person or event, force, or condition; in particular:
definitions.example: the youngest victims of the brutal war

definitions.definition: A living being which is slain and offered as a sacrifice, usually in a religious rite.
An unoccupied position or job.
An available room in a hotel; guest house, etc.
Empty space.
Lack of intelligence or understanding.
A defect in a crystal caused by the absence of an atom in a lattice
The hours of darkness between sunset and sunrise; the night.
Pertaining to nighttime; appropriate to the night.
Happening during the night.
To cut into something by clamping the teeth.
definitions.example: As soon as you bite that sandwich, you'll know how good it is.

definitions.definition: To hold something by clamping one's teeth.
To attack with the teeth.
definitions.example: That dog is about to bite!

definitions.definition: To behave aggressively; to reject advances.
definitions.example: If you see me, come and say hello. I don't bite.

definitions.definition: To take hold; to establish firm contact with.
definitions.example: I needed snow chains to make the tires bite.

definitions.definition: To have significant effect, often negative.
definitions.example: For homeowners with adjustable rate mortgages, rising interest will really bite.

definitions.definition: (of a fish) To bite a baited hook or other lure and thus be caught.
definitions.example: Are the fish biting today?

definitions.definition: To accept something offered, often secretly or deceptively, to cause some action by the acceptor.
definitions.example: I've planted the story. Do you think they'll bite?

definitions.definition: (of an insect) To sting.
definitions.example: These mosquitoes are really biting today!

definitions.definition: To cause a smarting sensation; to have a property which causes such a sensation; to be pungent.
definitions.example: It bites like pepper or mustard.

definitions.definition: (sometimes figurative) To cause sharp pain or damage to; to hurt or injure.
definitions.example: Pepper bites the mouth.

definitions.definition: To cause sharp pain; to produce anguish; to hurt or injure; to have the property of so doing.
To take or keep a firm hold.
definitions.example: The anchor bites.

definitions.definition: To take hold of; to hold fast; to adhere to.
definitions.example: The anchor bites the ground.

definitions.definition: To lack quality; to be worthy of derision; to suck.
definitions.example: This music really bites.

definitions.definition: To perform oral sex on. Used in invective.
definitions.example: You don't like that I sat on your car? Bite me.

definitions.definition: To plagiarize, to imitate.
definitions.example: He always be biting my moves.

definitions.definition: To deceive or defraud; to take in.

A round vessel or cask, of greater length than breadth, and bulging in the middle, made of staves bound with hoops, and having flat ends or heads. Sometimes applied to a similar cylindrical container made of metal, usually called a drum.
definitions.example: a cracker barrel

definitions.definition: The quantity which constitutes a full barrel: the volume or weight this represents varies by local law and custom.
A solid drum, or a hollow cylinder or case
definitions.example: the barrel of a windlass;  the barrel of a watch, within which the spring is coiled.

definitions.definition: A metallic tube, as of a gun, from which a projectile is discharged.
A tube.
The hollow basal part of a feather.
The part of a clarinet which connects the mouthpiece and upper joint, and looks rather like a barrel (1).
A wave that breaks with a hollow compartment.
A waste receptacle.
definitions.example: Throw it into the trash barrel.

definitions.definition: The ribs and belly of a horse or pony.
A jar.
Any of the dark-staining regions in the somatosensory cortex of rodents, etc., where somatosensory inputs from the contralateral side of the body come in from the thalamus.
A statistic derived from launch angle and exit velocity of a ball hit in play.
To put or to pack in a barrel or barrels.
To move quickly or in an uncontrolled manner.
definitions.example: He came barrelling around the corner and I almost hit him.

definitions.definition: A trip into any undeveloped area to see, photograph or hunt wild animals in their own environment.
A caravan going on a safari.
To take part in a safari.
In an open manner, visibly, not covertly.
(manner) In an incredible manner; not to be believed.
(degree) To a great extent; extremely.
definitions.example: He was incredibly wealthy.

definitions.definition: Used to note the surprising or hard-to-believe nature of what is being said and suggest that it is nevertheless true.
definitions.example: Incredibly, when he died they found he had left a million dollars to the Poetry Foundation.

definitions.definition: Unpleasantly sticky; yucky; disgusting.
definitions.example: I stepped in something icky and it smells terrible.

definitions.definition: Excessively sentimental.
Unwell or upset; in a bad state of mind or health.
definitions.example: I'm not going to work today. I just feel icky.

definitions.definition: Of the colour of ink, especially black ink; dark.
Spattered or stained with ink.
Dark-skinned; black.
Something brought in from an exterior source, especially for sale or trade.
The practice of importing.
Significance, importance.
definitions.example: It was a matter of great import.

definitions.definition: To bring (something) in from a foreign country, especially for sale or trade.
To load a file into a software application from another version or system.
definitions.example: How can I import files from older versions of this application?

definitions.definition: To be important; to be significant; to be of consequence.
To be of importance to (someone or something).
To be incumbent on (someone to do something).
To be important or crucial to (that something happen).
To mean, signify.
To express, to imply.
To return to life; to become reanimated or reinvigorated.
To return to life; to cause to recover life or strength; to cause to live anew.
definitions.example: Her grandmother refused to be revived if she lost consciousness.

definitions.definition: To recover from a state of oblivion, obscurity, neglect, or depression.
definitions.example: Classical learning revived in the fifteenth century.

definitions.definition: To restore, or bring again to life; to reanimate.
definitions.example: This new paint job should revive the surgery waiting room.

definitions.definition: To raise from coma, languor, depression, or discouragement; to bring into action after a suspension.
To renew in the mind or memory; to bring to recollection; to recall attention to; to reawaken.
definitions.example: The Harry Potter films revived the world's interest in wizardry

definitions.definition: To recover its natural or metallic state, as a metal.
To restore or reduce to its natural or metallic state
definitions.example: to revive a metal after calcination

definitions.definition: To exhibit a conspicuous (sometimes malevolent) pleasure or sense of self-satisfaction, often at an adversary's misfortune.
To triumph, crow, relish, glory, revel.
The quality of being decent; propriety.
That which is proper or becoming.
A voluntary gift or contribution for a specific cause.
definitions.example: They were collecting donations for the elderly at Christmas.

definitions.definition: The act of giving or bestowing; a grant.
Men's or boys' briefs.
(sometimes derogatory) Inclined towards the arts; arty.
Any one of the species of large odd-toed ungulates of the taxonomic family Tapiridae with a long prehensile upper lip, of which all four surviving species are in genus Tapirus.
Used to express anger, excitement, surprise, etc.
definitions.example: Blimey! I didn’t see that!

definitions.definition: A very tall building with a large number of floors.
A small sail atop a mast of a ship; a triangular skysail.
Anything very tall or high.
Having streaks.
Used to describe a shot where the ball deflects off the edge of the bat, but is not caught by the slips or wicket-keeper and instead results in runs for the batsman.
(chiefly of a person, usually North America) Having alternating periods of good and bad performances; inconsistent.
definitions.example: Jones has always been a streaky hitter

definitions.definition: Not well-executed, due to luck more than skill.
A horizontal component of a structure
Horizon
A Tasmanian shrub or small tree whose main trunk tends to lean over and grow horizontally, Anodopetalum biglandulosum
Perpendicular to the vertical; parallel to the plane of the horizon; level, flat
Relating to horizontal markets
Pertaining to the horizon
definitions.example: 1667: As when the Sun new ris'n / Looks through the Horizontal misty Air — John Milton, Paradise Lost, Book 1, ll. 594-5

definitions.definition: (wine tasting) involving wines of the same vintages but from different wineries
Of an interval: having the two notes sound successively.
In a heavy manner.
definitions.example: He clomped heavily up the stairs in his boots.

definitions.definition: With a great weight.
definitions.example: heavily burdened

definitions.definition: To a considerable degree, to a great extent.
definitions.example: He drank heavily.

definitions.definition: In a manner designed for heavy duty.
definitions.example: heavily armed soldiers;   heavily armoured tanks;   heavily reinforced walls

definitions.definition: So as to be thick or heavy.
definitions.example: heavily built young men;   his heavily muscled arms

definitions.definition: In a laboured manner.
definitions.example: he breathed heavily

definitions.definition: The act or instance of wandering.
definitions.example: to go for a wander in the park

definitions.definition: The situation where a value or signal etc. deviates from the correct or normal value.
definitions.example: baseline wander in ECG signals

definitions.definition: To move without purpose or specified destination; often in search of livelihood.
definitions.example: to wander over the fields

definitions.definition: To stray; stray from one's course; err.
definitions.example: A writer wanders from his subject.

definitions.definition: To commit adultery.
To go somewhere indirectly or at varying speeds; to move in a curved path.
Of the mind, to lose focus or clarity of argument or attention.
The branch of natural science that deals with the composition and constitution of substances and the changes that they undergo as a consequence of alterations in the constitution of their molecules.
An application of chemical theory and method to a particular substance.
The mutual attraction between two people; rapport.
Lawfulness.
Any small model of a person or animal able to be moved by strings or rods, or in the form of a glove.
A person, country, etc, controlled by another.
A poppet; a small image in the human form; a doll.
The upright support for the bearing of the spindle in a lathe.
In a fortunate manner.
It is (or was, etc) fortunate that.
definitions.example: Fortunately, Sam's pet hamster was not dead.

definitions.definition: A whirlwind, whirlpool, or similarly moving matter in the form of a spiral or column.
Anything that involves constant violent or chaotic activity around some centre.
definitions.example: 2004: the consumer vortex that is East Hampton — The New Yorker, 30 August 2004, p.38

definitions.definition: Anything that inevitably draws surrounding things into its current.

A supposed collection of particles of very subtle matter, endowed with a rapid rotary motion around an axis which was also the axis of a sun or planet; part of a Cartesian theory accounting for the formation of the universe, and the movements of the bodies composing it.
Any of numerous species of small Turbellaria belonging to Vortex and allied genera.
Any one out of an indefinite number of persons; anyone; any person.
definitions.example: Anybody will do.

definitions.definition: A person of some consideration or standing.
definitions.example: Everybody who wants to be anybody will come to Jake's party.

definitions.definition: A person appointed, or chosen, by parties to determine a controversy between them; an arbitrator.
(with of) A person or object having the power of judging and determining, or ordaining, without control; one whose power of deciding and governing is not limited.
definitions.example: Television and film, not Vogue and similar magazines, are the arbiters of fashion.

definitions.definition: A component in circuitry that allocates scarce resources.
To act as arbiter.
Something or someone that doesn't quite make it.
definitions.example: In all the submissions, they found four papers that were clearly worth publishing and another dozen almosts.

definitions.definition: Very close to, not quite.
definitions.example: Almost all people went there. - Not all but very close to it.

definitions.definition: A large slab of stone laid in a dry-stone wall from one side to the other; a perpend.
Passing from one side of something to the other.
definitions.example: Interstate highways form a nationwide system of through roads.

definitions.definition: Finished; complete.
definitions.example: They were through with laying the subroof by noon.

definitions.definition: Without a future; done for.
definitions.example: After being implicated in the scandal, he was through as an executive in financial services.

definitions.definition: No longer interested; wearied or turned off by experience.
definitions.example: She was through with him.

definitions.definition: Proceeding from origin to destination without delay due to change of equipment.
definitions.example: The through flight through Memphis was the fastest.

definitions.definition: In possession of the ball beyond the last line of defence but not necessarily the goalkeeper; through on goal.
From one side to the other by way of the interior.
definitions.example: The arrow went straight through.

definitions.definition: From one end to the other.
definitions.example: Others slept; he worked straight through.

definitions.definition: To the end.
definitions.example: He said he would see it through.

definitions.definition: Completely.
definitions.example: Leave the yarn in the dye overnight so the color soaks through.

definitions.definition: Out into the open.
definitions.example: The American army broke through at St. Lo.

definitions.definition: From one side of an opening to the other.
definitions.example: I went through the window.

definitions.definition: Entering, then later leaving.
definitions.example: I drove through the town at top speed without looking left or right.

definitions.definition: Surrounded by (while moving).
definitions.example: We slogged through the mud for hours before turning back and giving up.

definitions.definition: By means of.
definitions.example: This team believes in winning through intimidation.

definitions.definition: In consequence of; as a result of.
To (or up to) and including, with all intermediate values.
definitions.example: from 1945 through 1991;  the numbers 1 through 9;  your membership is active through March 15, 2013

definitions.definition: A visible, narrow, and intense (relative to ambient light) ray of sunlight.
An item of cutlery or crockery laid out on a table, but not used, and which can be returned to the drawer without being washed.
Any of various lycaenid butterflies of the genus Curetis.
Any hummingbird of the genus Aglaeactis.
The direct rays, light or warmth of the sun.
definitions.example: We were warmed by the bright sunshine.

definitions.definition: A location on which the sun's rays fall.
definitions.example: We moved out of the shade and into the sunshine.

definitions.definition: Geniality or cheerfulness.
definitions.example: I enjoyed the sunshine of her smile.

definitions.definition: A source of cheerfulness or joy.
The effect which the sun has when it lights and warms some place.
Friendly form of address often reserved for juniors.
definitions.example: Alright sunshine, safe to cross now.

definitions.definition: Ironic form of address used to an inferior or troublemaker.
definitions.example: OK, sunshine, listen up and listen good. There's five vandalised telephone boxes out there and I know you're responsible.

definitions.definition: Used to address someone who has just woken up and/or is very sleepy.
definitions.example: Good morning, sunshine!

definitions.definition: A covering for the head, often in the approximate form of a cone or a cylinder closed at its top end, and sometimes having a brim and other decoration.
A particular role or capacity that a person might fill.
Any receptacle from which numbers/names are pulled out in a lottery.
A hat switch.
The háček symbol.
The caret symbol ^.
User rights on a website, such as the right to edit pages others cannot.
(Cambridge University slang) A student who is also the son of a nobleman (and so allowed to wear a hat instead of a mortarboard).
To place a hat on.
To appoint as cardinal.
At once; without delay.
definitions.example: When the neighbours' dog barked, ours instantly replied with a howl.

definitions.definition: Urgently; with insistence.
At the same time.
The movement of a vessel from land into the water; especially, the sliding on ways from the stocks on which it is built. (Compare: to splash a ship.)
The act or fact of launching (a ship/vessel, a project, a new book, etc.).
An event held to celebrate the launch of a ship/vessel, project, a new book, etc.; a launch party.
definitions.example: book launch

definitions.definition: To throw (a projectile such as a lance, dart or ball); to hurl; to propel with force.
To pierce with, or as with, a lance.
To cause (a vessel) to move or slide from the land or a larger vessel into the water; to set afloat.
definitions.example: The navy launched another ship.

definitions.definition: To cause (a rocket, balloon, etc., or the payload thereof) to begin its flight upward from the ground.
definitions.example: NASA launched several unmanned rockets before launching any of the Mercury astronauts.

definitions.definition: To send out; to start (someone) on a mission or project; to give a start to (something); to put in operation
definitions.example: Our business launched a new project.

definitions.definition: To start (a program or feature); to execute or bring into operation.
definitions.example: Double-click an icon to launch the associated application.

definitions.definition: To release; to put onto the market for sale
Of a ship, rocket, balloon, etc.: to depart on a voyage; to take off.
(often with out) To move with force and swiftness like a sliding from the stocks into the water; to plunge; to begin.
definitions.example: to launch into an argument or discussion

definitions.definition: (of a program) To start to operate.
definitions.example: After clicking the icon, the application will launch.

definitions.definition: A transport system using rails used to move passengers or goods.
A track, consisting of parallel rails, over which wheeled vehicles such as trains may travel.
Indefinitely large numerically, many.
definitions.example: There are numerous definitions of the word 'man'.

definitions.definition: A precious or semi-precious stone; gem, gemstone.
A valuable object used for personal ornamentation, especially one made of precious metals and stones; a piece of jewellery.
Anything precious or valuable.
definitions.example: Galveston was the jewel of Texas prior to the hurricane.

definitions.definition: A bearing for a pivot in a watch, formed of a crystal or precious stone.
Any of various lycaenid butterflies of the genus Hypochrysops.
The clitoris.
To bejewel; to decorate or bedeck with jewels or gems.
Family jewels; testicles.
A robot that is designed to look and act like a human being (not necessarily male)
Possessing human qualities.
(in pelvimetry) Of the pelvis, having a narrow anterior segment and a heart-shaped brim, typically found in the male.
The state of a society being without authorities or an authoritative governing body.
Anarchism; the political theory that a community is best organized by the voluntary cooperation of individuals, rather than by a government, which is regarded as being coercive by nature.
A chaotic and confusing absence of any form of political authority or government.
Confusion in general; disorder.
Worthy of respect; respectable.

A courtesy title, given in Britain and the Commonwealth to a cabinet minister, minister of state, or senator, and in the United States to the president, vice president, congresspeople, state governors and legislators, and mayors.
A branched, often ornate, lighting fixture suspended from the ceiling
(auction) A fictional bidder used to increase the price at an auction.
A portable frame used to support temporary wooden fences.
An endoilluminator used in eye surgery.
A group or association of cooperating members.

definitions.definition: An organization of sports teams which play against one another for a championship.
definitions.example: My favorite sports organizations are the National Football League and the American League in baseball.

definitions.definition: (often in the negative) A class or type of people or things that are evenly matched or on the same level.
definitions.example: Forget about dating him; he's out of your league.

definitions.definition: A prefecture-level administrative unit in Inner Mongolia (Chinese: 盟).
To form an association; to unite in a league or confederacy; to combine for mutual support.
A version of rugby football, played between two teams of thirteen.
The story or truth.
definitions.example: All the reporters hoped to be the first to get the lowdown on the celebrity's marriage plans.

definitions.definition: Unfair; shameful.
definitions.example: lowdown tactics

definitions.definition: A spasmodic, nervous system disease brought on by the tetanus bacteria. It causes muscles to seize up and may cause death by suffocation.

A branching-out, the act or result of developing branches; specifically the divergence of the stem and limbs of a plant into smaller ones, or of similar developments in blood vessels, anatomical structures etc.
An offshoot of a decision, fact etc.; a consequence or implication, especially one which complicates a situation.
An arrangement of branches.
A bundle of neurons with their connective tissue sheaths, blood vessels and lymphatics.

definitions.definition: A neuron.
A vein in a leaf; a grain in wood
definitions.example: Some plants have ornamental value because of their contrasting nerves.

definitions.definition: Courage, boldness.
definitions.example: He hasn't the nerve to tell her he likes her. What a wimp!

definitions.definition: Patience.
definitions.example: The web-team found git-sed is really a time and nerve saver when doing mass changes on your repositories

definitions.definition: Stamina, endurance, fortitude.
Audacity, gall.
definitions.example: He had the nerve to enter my house uninvited.

definitions.definition: (polymer technology) The elastic resistance of raw rubber or other polymers to permanent deformation during processing.
(in the plural) Agitation caused by fear, stress or other negative emotion.
definitions.example: Ellie had a bad case of nerves before the big test.

definitions.definition: Sinew, tendon.
To give courage.
definitions.example: May their example nerve us to face the enemy.

definitions.definition: To give strength; to supply energy or vigour.
definitions.example: The liquor nerved up several of the men after their icy march.

definitions.definition: Extreme pain, either of body or mind; excruciating distress.
A warm waterproof overshoe.
Any of various butterflies of the genus Oeneis.
(now only in compounds) Pertaining to the celestial north pole, or to the pole star.
Pertaining to the northern polar region of the planet, characterised by extreme cold and an icy landscape.
Extremely cold, snowy, or having other properties of extreme winter associated with the Arctic.
Designed for use in very cold conditions.
One blade on a pair of scissors.
Scissors.
(noun adjunct) Used in certain noun phrases to denote a thing resembling the action of scissors, as scissor kick, scissor hold (wrestling), scissor jack.
To cut using, or as if using, scissors.
To excise or expunge something from a text.

definitions.definition: To reproduce (text) as an excerpt, copy.
To move something like a pair of scissors, especially the legs.

definitions.definition: (sex) To engage in scissoring (tribadism), a sexual act in which two women intertwine their legs and rub their vulvas against each other.
To skate with one foot significantly in front of the other.
